Recent updates from New York include a rabid coyote attack, potential norovirus contamination in seafood, and a federal judge's ruling affecting the Gateway Tunnel project, alongside ongoing discussions about NYC's homeless population and safety measures for subway crews.

### Risks & Alerts

- Rabid coyote attacks 3 people and 6 dogs north of NYC; residents advised to stay vigilant. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Seafood sold in NY may be contaminated with norovirus; consumers urged to check sources before purchasing. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- FDNY battles duct fire at New Hudson Yards restaurant Saverne; no injuries reported. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMigwFBVV95cUxQeFlBcGlGSmloNlFzeS11Z1ZRR1p3OTRRQmZzeTNBcmFaeXZobmFBR0FNLWJkaEdhRTluazdqanVnblJVYzg2RW4ya0FHZ1c2bExGZFdfZ0hjY3RtcTJTZ3kyNzFFOUJGWkIzZ1JBNklrbU1wbi1mUnNzUHZQSlJPVy1PSQ?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- New York court clears way for NYC foie gras ban to take effect.
